description:
John Zenon, a 34-year-old auto body spray painter, presents to the occupational health clinic with progressive shortness of breath, chest tightness, and wheezing associated with workplace chemical exposure (diisocyanates). Initial triage vitals show a Respiratory Rate of 24 breaths per minute and an Oxygen Saturation (SpO2) of 93% on room air. As the occupational health professional, document this workplace illness in GNU Health by recording an asthma diagnosis with the appropriate ICD-10 code (J45 or subcode) as an active condition and creating a clinical evaluation capturing the respiratory distress vitals. Prescribe a rescue inhaler (such as Salbutamol, Albuterol, Budesonide, Fluticasone, or Beclometasone), order at least two baseline laboratory tests for the immunological workup (e.g., CBC and IgE), and schedule a pulmonology follow-up appointment for between 14 to 30 days from today.
